headline: Boston Properties (BXP) Stock Looks Cheap On Cash Flow But Fair On Earnings
summary: BXP stock has returned 38.5% over the past three years, and the latest Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate points to the shares trading at roughly a 20.8% discount, while the broader valuation checks still flag the company as only a low-score value case. That mix of signals leaves investors weighing a DCF that hints at upside against a framework of other checks that suggests BXP is not a straightforward bargain. Over the last three years, BXP has delivered a 38.5% return,...
